explanation_en: 'Verhältnis' means 'relationship' or 'ratio'. It is a neuter noun taking 'das'.

question.de: _____ Verhältnis zwischen den beiden ist gut.
question.en: The relationship between the two is good.

explanation_en: 'Verhältnis' is neuter. As the nominative subject, it takes 'das'.

question.de: Das _____ von Arbeit zu Freizeit ist wichtig.
question.en: The ratio of work to leisure time is important.

explanation_en: 'Verhältnis' means 'ratio' here. It is the neuter subject, used with 'das'.

question.en: We need to improve the relationship.

explanation_en: 'verbessern' takes the accusative. Neuter: 'das Verhältnis' (unchanged in accusative).
'Ergebnis' means 'result'. It is a neuter noun taking 'das'.

question.en: The result was better than expected.

explanation_en: 'Ergebnis' is neuter. As the nominative subject, it takes 'das'.

question.de: Das _____ der Prüfung kommt nächste Woche.
question.en: The result of the exam comes next week.

explanation_en: 'Ergebnis' means 'result'. It is the neuter subject, used with 'das'.

question.de: Wir warten auf das _____ der Untersuchung.
question.en: We are waiting for the result of the investigation.

explanation_en: 'warten auf' takes the accusative. Neuter: 'das Ergebnis' (unchanged in accusative).
'Beispiel' means 'example'. It is a neuter noun taking 'das'.

question.en: The example explains the rule well.

explanation_en: 'Beispiel' is neuter. As the nominative subject, it takes 'das'.

question.en: The example makes the difference clear.

explanation_en: 'Beispiel' means 'example'. It is the neuter subject, used with 'das'.

question.en: Can you give me an example?

explanation_en: After 'ein' (neuter indefinite accusative), 'Beispiel' is unchanged — neuter nominative and accusative are identical.
'Angebot' means 'offer' or 'special offer'. It is a neuter noun taking 'das'.

question.en: The offer is very tempting.

explanation_en: 'Angebot' is neuter. As the nominative subject, it takes 'das'.

question.de: Das _____ gilt bis Ende des Monats.
question.en: The offer is valid until the end of the month.

explanation_en: 'Angebot' means 'offer'. It is the neuter subject, used with 'das'.

question.en: I gladly accept the offer.

explanation_en: 'annehmen' takes the accusative. Neuter: 'das Angebot' (unchanged in accusative).
'Interesse' means 'interest'. It is a neuter noun taking 'das'.

question.en: The interest in art is growing.

explanation_en: 'Interesse' is neuter. As the nominative subject, it takes 'das'.

question.de: Das _____ an diesem Thema ist groß.
question.en: The interest in this topic is great.

explanation_en: 'Interesse' means 'interest'. It is the neuter subject here.

question.en: He shows great interest in music.

explanation_en: After 'großes' (neuter accusative adjective), 'Interesse' is unchanged — neuter nominative and accusative are identical.
'Verhalten' means 'behaviour'. It is a neuter noun taking 'das'.

question.en: The pupil's behaviour is strange.

explanation_en: 'Verhalten' is neuter. As the nominative subject, it takes 'das'.

question.de: Das _____ der Kinder ist manchmal schwer zu verstehen.
question.en: Children's behaviour is sometimes hard to understand.

explanation_en: 'Verhalten' means 'behaviour'. It is the neuter subject, used with 'das'.

question.en: We need to discuss his behaviour.

explanation_en: 'besprechen' takes the accusative. Neuter: 'sein Verhalten' (unchanged in accusative).
'Ziel' means 'goal', 'aim', or 'destination'. It is a neuter noun taking 'das'.

question.en: The goal is clearly defined.

explanation_en: 'Ziel' is neuter. As the nominative subject, it takes 'das'.

question.de: Das _____ des Projekts ist mehr Nachhaltigkeit.
question.en: The goal of the project is more sustainability.

explanation_en: 'Ziel' means 'goal'. It is the neuter subject, used with 'das'.

question.de: Ich verfolge mein _____ mit großer Ausdauer.
question.en: I pursue my goal with great perseverance.

explanation_en: 'verfolgen' takes the accusative. 'mein Ziel' — neuter possessive accusative: 'mein' (unchanged). Noun also unchanged.
'Thema' means 'topic' or 'theme'. It is a neuter noun taking 'das'.

question.en: The topic is very current.

explanation_en: 'Thema' is neuter. As the nominative subject, it takes 'das'.

question.de: Das _____ des Aufsatzes ist die Umwelt.
question.en: The topic of the essay is the environment.

explanation_en: 'Thema' means 'topic'. It is the neuter subject, used with 'das'.

question.de: Wir wählen ein neues _____ für das Projekt.
question.en: We are choosing a new topic for the project.

explanation_en: After 'ein neues' (neuter indefinite accusative adjective), 'Thema' is unchanged.
'Recht' means 'right' (entitlement) or 'law'. It is a neuter noun taking 'das'.

question.de: _____ Recht auf Bildung gilt für alle.
question.en: The right to education applies to everyone.

explanation_en: 'Recht' is neuter. As the nominative subject, it takes 'das'.

question.en: The right to freedom of expression is important.

explanation_en: 'Recht' means 'right'. It is the neuter subject, used with 'das'.

question.de: Jeder Bürger hat das _____, zu wählen.
question.en: Every citizen has the right to vote.

explanation_en: 'hat das Recht' — 'haben' takes accusative. Neuter: 'das Recht' (unchanged in accusative).
'Gebiet' means 'area', 'field', or 'territory'. It is a neuter noun taking 'das'.

question.en: The area is very large.

explanation_en: 'Gebiet' is neuter. As the nominative subject, it takes 'das'.

question.de: Das _____ der Medizin entwickelt sich schnell.
question.en: The field of medicine is developing rapidly.

explanation_en: 'Gebiet' means 'field'. It is the neuter subject, used with 'das'.

question.en: I know this area very well.

explanation_en: 'kennen' takes the accusative. Neuter: 'dieses Gebiet' (neuter accusative demonstrative 'dieses', noun unchanged).
'Einfluss' means 'influence'. It is a masculine noun taking 'der'.

question.en: The influence of the media is enormous.

explanation_en: 'Einfluss' is masculine. As the nominative subject, it takes 'der'.

question.de: Der _____ dieser Person auf die Gruppe ist groß.
question.en: This person's influence on the group is great.

explanation_en: 'Einfluss' means 'influence'. It is the masculine subject, used with 'der'.

question.de: Er nutzt seinen _____ für gute Zwecke.
question.en: He uses his influence for good purposes.

explanation_en: 'nutzen' takes the accusative. 'seinen Einfluss' — masculine accusative uses 'seinen'. Noun is unchanged.
'Unterschied' means 'difference'. It is a masculine noun taking 'der'.

question.en: The difference is small but important.

explanation_en: 'Unterschied' is masculine. As the nominative subject, it takes 'der'.

question.de: Der _____ zwischen den zwei Wörtern ist subtil.
question.en: The difference between the two words is subtle.

explanation_en: 'Unterschied' means 'difference'. It is the masculine subject, used with 'der'.

question.de: Ich sehe keinen _____ zwischen den beiden Optionen.
question.en: I see no difference between the two options.

explanation_en: 'sehen' takes the accusative. 'keinen Unterschied' — masculine accusative uses 'keinen'.
'Zusammenhang' means 'connection' or 'context'. It is a masculine noun taking 'der'.

question.de: _____ Zusammenhang ist wichtig für das Verständnis.
question.en: The context is important for understanding.

explanation_en: 'Zusammenhang' is masculine. As the nominative subject, it takes 'der'.

question.de: Der _____ zwischen den Ereignissen ist klar.
question.en: The connection between the events is clear.

explanation_en: 'Zusammenhang' means 'connection'. It is the masculine subject, used with 'der'.

question.en: I don't quite understand the context.

explanation_en: 'verstehen' takes the accusative. 'den Zusammenhang' — masculine accusative uses 'den'. Noun is unchanged.
'Bereich' means 'area', 'field', or 'sector'. It is a masculine noun taking 'der'.

question.de: _____ Bereich der Bildung braucht mehr Geld.
question.en: The field of education needs more money.

explanation_en: 'Bereich' is masculine. As the nominative subject, it takes 'der'.

question.de: In diesem _____ habe ich viel Erfahrung.
question.en: In this field I have a lot of experience.

explanation_en: 'Bereich' means 'field' or 'area'. After 'diesem' (dative, masculine), the noun is unchanged.

question.en: We need to further develop this sector.

explanation_en: 'weiterentwickeln' takes the accusative. 'diesen Bereich' — masculine accusative uses 'diesen'. Noun unchanged.
